data.title: Analyst predicts Bitcoin will continue to decline against stock market as cycle ends. Bitcoiners seeking shift from gold, silver might be mistaken.
data.description: According to Benjamin Cowen, those holding onto hopes of a significant shift from gold and silver to Bitcoin may be focusing on the wrong indicator.

data.title: Analyst warns that SoFi is slightly overvalued at $24, with fair value closer to $20; company added 905,000 new members.
data.description: Analyst Parkev Tatevosian, CFA, expressed caution regarding SoFi Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: SOFI) ahead of its fourth-quarter results. Despite previously being bullish on the stock, Tatevosian now considers it slightly overvalued at its current levels. He believes SoFi is not an attractive purchase before earnings due to increased volatility and limited upside compared to risk. According to his models, the stock's value is approximately $20 per share, with precise valuation at $19.60. While the current price is not deemed extremely overvalued, Tatevosian still views it as slightly overvalued when considering a margin of safety. This assessment comes despite the company's solid operating performances, which saw the addition of 905,000 new members in the third quarter. For further details, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Frontera sells Colombian E&P assets to GeoPark for $622 million, retains infrastructure business and pays shareholders $25 million.
data.description: Frontera has decided to maintain its infrastructure division due to a consistent dividend flow from its involvement in ODL. It also boasts a promising growth outlook in Puerto Bahia. After finalizing the transaction, shareholders will receive cash, along with an additional contingent payment of $25 million.

data.title: Investors nervous as companies pour huge money into A.I., waiting for results from Meta and Microsoft.
data.description: Tech giants such as Microsoft and Meta continue to invest heavily in artificial intelligence technology, with expenditures reaching significant amounts. However, despite the substantial funds allocated to AI, investors are apprehensive about witnessing tangible outcomes from these investments.

data.title: Lukoil discussing asset sale to U.S. firm Carlyle amid sanctions; hit to Kremlin’s oil revenue.
data.description: Following sanctions imposed by both the United States and the European Union on Lukoil, a significant blow was dealt to the Kremlin's oil revenue. In response, a deal with Carlyle has been finalized. This development underscores the impact of the sanctions, with Lukoil being a major player in the industry. The financial terms of the deal remain undisclosed, but it signifies a strategic move in the face of geopolitical pressures affecting oil markets and related revenue streams. The agreement reflects the complex dynamics shaping global energy transactions amid a backdrop of political tensions and economic considerations.

data.title: Newark Museum of Art announces new leader for the institution.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global economy is expected to grow by 4.4% in 2021, up from the previous forecast of 3.6%. This positive outlook is attributed to the rapid pace of COVID-19 vaccinations and unprecedented government support measures. The United States economy is projected to expand by 6.4% this year, the fastest pace since 1984. China, the world's second largest economy, is forecasted to grow by 8.4% in 2021. However, the recovery remains uneven, with emerging markets and developing economies facing significant challenges. Inflation concerns are also on the rise, with consumer prices expected to increase by 2.6% globally this year. As the world continues to navigate the repercussions of the pandemic, policymakers are cautiously optimistic about the economic outlook.

data.title: Trump's trade policies create winners and losers for manufacturers, with tariffs impacting parts and materials used in finished goods.
data.description: Tariffs have provided support for a few businesses, yet they have primarily impacted the components and resources essential for the production of finished products.
Cold states are reducing costs for running heat pumps to promote energy efficiency.

data.title: Rug maker survived 1 year of Trump tariffs but faces new challenges. Inventory low after stockpiling from India, Turkey.
data.description: Loloi has amassed a collection of rugs from various countries including India and Turkey, but their stock is depleting rapidly.

data.title: Amid bustling activity at China's Ningbo port, the local housing market crashes and restaurants remain deserted.
data.description: In China’s bustling port city of Ningbo, there is a constant flow of shipping traffic and factories in operation. However, despite this activity, the local housing market has plummeted, with an unprecedented crash in prices. The once vibrant restaurants in the area now remain largely empty, reflecting the significant economic downturn experienced in the region.

data.title: Starmer and Xi discuss economic ties amid US tensions, potentially boosting UK-China relations.
data.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is aiming to enhance economic relations for development by extending an invitation to Chinese President Xi Jinping. This move signals an opportunity for China to engage with one of the key allies of the United States.

data.title: Trump to announce Kevin Warsh as new Fed Chair, replacing Jerome H. Powell. Source says Warsh is well-known in financial circles.
data.description: The President is preparing to reveal his pick to succeed Jerome H. Powell as Federal Reserve chair, citing the nominee's widespread recognition in the financial sector.

data.title: BTC drops to $81K, causing $1.7B liquidations amid geopolitical tensions, tariff threats, and tech earnings worries.
data.description: The value of Bitcoin dropped to its lowest level in nine months due to worries over geopolitical tensions, threats of tariffs, and tech companies' earnings reports, causing traders to liquidate long positions amounting to billions of dollars.
